title: CIRCT integration tests failed (FSM variable test)
body:
At first, I wanted to install firtool in WSL (Arch). I install it from [AUR](https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/firtool). It can build successfully, but integration tests will fail. So, I try to follow the README and build the master branch of circt manually. And it's integration tests also failed.

Below is the test results (I use the RELEASE build type, because my machine may not have enough memory to build it in DEBUG. Other config is same to the README):
```sh
❯ ninja check-circt-integration
[0/1] Running the CIRCT integration tests
FAIL: CIRCT :: Dialect/FSM/variable/top.mlir (66 of 71)
******************** TEST 'CIRCT :: Dialect/FSM/variable/top.mlir' FAILED ********************
Script:
--
: 'RUN: at line 2';   /home/alkaid/source/circt/build/bin/circt-opt /home/alkaid/source/circt/integration_test/Dialect/FSM/variable/top.mlir --convert-fsm-to-sv --canonicalize --lower-seq-to-sv --export-split-verilog -o /home/alkaid/source/circt/build/integration_test/Dialect/FSM/variable/Output/top.mlir.tmp2.mlir
: 'RUN: at line 3';   /home/alkaid/source/circt/build/bin/circt-rtl-sim.py --compileargs="-I/home/alkaid/source/circt/build/integration_test/Dialect/FSM/variable/Output/.." top.sv /home/alkaid/source/circt/integration_test/Dialect/FSM/variable/driver.cpp --no-default-driver | /home/alkaid/source/circt/llvm/build/bin/FileCheck /home/alkaid/source/circt/integration_test/Dialect/FSM/variable/top.mlir
--
Exit Code: 1

Command Output (stderr):
--
/home/alkaid/source/circt/integration_test/Dialect/FSM/variable/top.mlir:9:16: error: CHECK-NEXT: is not on the line after the previous match
// CHECK-NEXT: out: 0
               ^
<stdin>:20:1: note: 'next' match was here
out: 0
^
<stdin>:14:7: note: previous match ended here
out: 0
      ^
<stdin>:15:1: note: non-matching line after previous match is here
out: 1
^

Input file: <stdin>
Check file: /home/alkaid/source/circt/integration_test/Dialect/FSM/variable/top.mlir

-dump-input=help explains the following input dump.

Input was:
<<<<<<
        .
        .
        .
       15: out: 1
       16: out: 2
       17: out: 3
       18: out: 4
       19: out: 5
       20: out: 0
next:9     !~~~~~  error: match on wrong line
       21: out: 0
>>>>>>

--

********************
********************
Failed Tests (1):
  CIRCT :: Dialect/FSM/variable/top.mlir


Testing Time: 3.37s
  Unsupported: 38
  Passed     : 32
  Failed     :  1
FAILED: integration_test/CMakeFiles/check-circt-integration /home/alkaid/source/circt/build/integration_test/CMakeFiles/check-circt-integration
cd /home/alkaid/source/circt/build/integration_test && /usr/sbin/python3.10 /home/alkaid/source/circt/llvm/build/./bin/llvm-lit -sv /home/alkaid/source/circt/build/integration_test
ninja: build stopped: subcommand failed.
```
